column12: Measurement condition : Ice; Mostly Cloudy , No Precipitation , Calm Wind Blowing Downstream
column13: ICE COVERAGE ALONG MOST OF RIVER BETWEEN GAUGE AND MMNT SECTION, WITH THE EXCEPTION OF RAPID SECTIONS. SIGNIFICANT AMOUNT OF WATER ON TOP OF ICE FOLLOWING THE DRILLING OF MMNT HOLES. AMOUNT OF WATER OVER ICE APPEARED TO INCREASE AS TIME PASSED AFTER THE MMNT HOLES WERE DRILLED.
column14: MMNT COLLECTED APPROX 500 M D/S OF GAUGE. RIVER HAD MULTIPLE ICE LAYERS AND BY THE END OF MEASURMENT, SIGNIFICANT WATER LAYER FLOWING OVER THE ICE. EPHEMERAL FLOW, WITH WATER PRESSURE PUSHING WATER UP ICE HOLE RESULTING IN OVER ICE FLOW OCCURRED IN EACH HOLE. IN ADDITION, FIRST TWOHOLES DRILLED ON RR WERE ICE TO BOTTOM, COULD HEAR WATER FLOWING U/S ON RR, BUT ICE TO BOTTOM HOLES INDICATED THAT THE WATER MAY BE CHANNELLED TOWARDS THE CENTER AT POINT OF TRANSECT. WATER APPEARED OVER ICE THE LENGTH OF STREAM BETWEEN MMNT SECTION AND GAUGE. UPON DEPARTURE, IT WAS EVIDENT THAT MORE WATER WAS FLOWING OVER ICE. AS A RESULT, IT  IS  LIKELY THIS MMNT IS AN UNDER ESTIMATE.    TWO COMPLETE ICE LAYERS OCCURED ALONG THE MMNT TRANSECT.  THREE HOLES WERE MEASURED FOR TOP ICE LAYER THICKNESS AS WELL AS EXTENT OF WATER LAYER DEPTH SITTING ATOP OF MAIN ICE LAYER. 1) ON RR, THE ICE THICKNESS WAS 0.140m AND THE EFFECTIVE WATER LAYER WAS 0.080m 2) IN THE MIDDLE, THE ICE THICKNESS WAS 0.200m AND THE EFFECTIVE WATER LAYER WAS 0.200m 3) ON RL, THE ICE THICKNESS WAS 0.200m AND THE EFFECTIVE WATER LAYER WAS 0.140m WHEN THE MMNT WAS COMPLETED, TECHNICANS ATTEMPTED TO DO A QUICK MMNT OF THE EFFECTIVE WATER LAYER TRAPPED BETWEEN THE TWO ICE LAYERS IN ONE OF THE MMNT HOLES ALONG THE TRANSECT. THE VELOCITIY ANGLES DURING THE TEST WERE VARIABLE, WITH A NEGATIVE VELOCITY RESULTING. TECHNICIANS CONSIDER THIS LAYER AS MINIMAL TO NOT FLOWING.  @ Uncertainty: IVE method, 2-sigma value (2 x Uncertainty Value reported in *.ft File). @ ISSUE WITH SL2 COMMUNICATION CORD DELAYED CHECK OF DWL TO LOGGED MMNT AT 1235. NEW CABLE HAD TO BE GRABBED FROM HELICOPTER  OFFSET UPON ARRIVAL = 1.752, RS AT 1246, NEW OFFSET = 1.770, RS AT 1303 NEW OFFSET = 1.786, TECH REALIZED THE ORIGINAL RS AT 1246 HAD THE WRONG CALCULATED OFFSET. THIS IS A FAIRLY LARGE SRC FOR THIS LOCATION, HOWEVER THIS SITE HAS NOT BEEN VISITED SINCE SEPT 2023. LEVELS WERE RUN WITH TWO INDEPENDENT SET UPS.  NOTE TO RFT = PLEASE SURPRESS LOGGED VALUES FROM 1236 TILL 1304, ORIGINAL RS HAD INCORRECTLY CALCULATED OFFSET, DWL TO NEW CORRECTED OFFSET OF 1.786 CONFIRMED WITHIN 0.003m AT 1305. IN ADDITION, A DWL WAS NOT TAKEN PRIOR TO THE MMNT AS THIS LOCATION REQUIRES REPOSITION OF HELICOPTER BETWEEN WINTER MMNT SECTION AND GAUGING LOCATION.  Mid-section

Comp lead has recalculated the cMGH as follows; average of 2021-06-11 logged values from 1400-1440 = 2.609m + SRC (-0.010m) = 2.599m. The eHSN had a recorded cMGH of 2.594m with an SRC 0f -0.014m  having been applied. An adj of +0.001m has been added within AQ to align with comp leads calculations.
